data.content:
Reentrancy Attack
Reentrancy Attack, also known as recursive call attack, involves a malicious contract calling back into the calling contract before the first invocation of the function is finished, potentially causing undesirable interactions.

CWE-841
CWE-841 refers to Improper Enforcement of Behavioral Workflow, highlighting a weakness in managing control flow in software.

Checks-Effects-Interactions Pattern
Checks-Effects-Interactions Pattern is a best practice in smart contract development to prevent reentrancy attacks by ensuring all internal state changes occur before external calls.

ReentrancyGuard
ReentrancyGuard, like OpenZeppelin's ReentrancyGuard, is a reentrancy lock used to prevent reentrancy vulnerabilities in smart contracts.

ModifierEntrancy
ModifierEntrancy is a smart contract that demonstrates a reentrancy vulnerability in its original form (modifier_reentrancy.sol) and a fix in a later version (modifier_reentrancy_fixed.sol).

Bank
Bank is a smart contract used in conjunction with ModifierEntrancy to simulate a scenario where reentrancy vulnerabilities can occur.

Nu Token
Nu Token is a token referenced within the smart contracts used to demonstrate reentrancy vulnerabilities.

SimpleDAO
SimpleDAO is a smart contract vulnerable to reentrancy attacks, as demonstrated in simple_dao.sol, and a fixed version, as shown in simple_dao_fixed.sol.

data.entity_name: Ethereum Smart Contract Best Practices - Reentrancy
data.content:
Ethereum Smart Contract Best Practices - Reentrancy
Ethereum Smart Contract Best Practices - Reentrancy is a resource providing guidelines on avoiding reentrancy vulnerabilities in Ethereum smart contracts.

Atzei N.
Atzei N. is an author credited in the source code of SimpleDAO smart contract.

Bartoletti M.
Bartoletti M. is an author credited in the source code of SimpleDAO smart contract.

Cimoli T
Cimoli T is an author credited in the source code of SimpleDAO smart contract.

Josselin Feist
Josselin Feist is a person who modified the SimpleDAO smart contract.

Bernhard Mueller
Bernhard Mueller is a person who modified the SimpleDAO smart contract.

Credit
Credit is a data that stores the credit balance of an address.

To
To is the recipient address for donations.

Msg.value
Msg.value represents the amount of Ether being sent in a transaction.

Amount
Amount is the amount of Ether to be withdrawn.

Msg.sender
Msg.sender is the address of the account initiating the transaction.<SEP>Msg.sender is the address of the account that sent the transaction.<SEP>Msg.sender refers to the address that initiated the transaction.

Address
Address is a data that represents an Ethereum address.<SEP>Address refers to an Ethereum address, identifying accounts and contracts.

Donate
The donate function accepts Ether from any address and increases the recipient's credit.

Withdraw
The withdraw function allows an address to withdraw Ether if they have sufficient credit.

QueryCredit
The queryCredit function allows anyone to check the credit balance of any address.

Floating Pragma
Floating Pragma is a concept related to Solidity compiler versioning in smart contract development.

CWE-664
CWE-664 refers to Improper Control of a Resource Through its Lifetime, a software weakness that can affect smart contract security.

Eea Ethtrust Security Levels
EEA EthTrust Security Levels are a set of standards related to compiler improvements and security bugs in Ethereum smart contracts.

Level [S] Improved Compilers
Level [S] Improved Compilers is an EEA EthTrust Security Level focused on compiler improvements.

Level [S] Compiler Security Bugs
Level [S] Compiler Security Bugs is an EEA EthTrust Security Level focused on addressing compiler security bugs.

data.entity_name: Level [M] Compiler Bugs And Overriding Requirements
data.content:
Level [M] Compiler Bugs And Overriding Requirements
Level [M] Compiler Bugs and Overriding Requirements is an EEA EthTrust Security Level concerning compiler bugs and overriding requirements.

data.entity_name: Recommended Practice Use The Latest Compiler
data.content:
Recommended Practice Use The Latest Compiler
Recommended Practice Use the Latest Compiler advises using the latest compiler version for smart contract development.

Compiler
The Compiler is used to translate Solidity code into executable bytecode, and its version affects contract security and functionality.

Solidity
Solidity is a programming language used for writing smart contracts.<SEP>A programming language used for writing smart contracts on blockchain platforms.

Ethpm
EthPM is a package manager for Ethereum smart contracts.

Github
GitHub is a web-based platform used for version control and collaborative software development.

Ethereum
Ethereum is a blockchain platform.

PragmaNotLocked Contract
PragmaNotLocked Contract is a sample contract without a locked pragma version.

PragmaFixed Contract
PragmaFixed Contract is a sample contract with a fixed pragma version.

SemVerFloatingPragma Contract
SemVerFloatingPragma Contract is a sample contract with a floating pragma version.

SemVerFloatingPragmaFixed Contract
SemVerFloatingPragmaFixed Contract is a sample contract with a fixed pragma version.

Outdated Compiler Version
Refers to the use of a compiler version that is not the most recent, potentially leading to bugs and vulnerabilities.

CWE-937
CWE-937 refers to using components with known vulnerabilities, including outdated compilers.

EEA EthTrust Security Levels
EEA EthTrust Security Levels are guidelines that address compiler improvements and security bugs related to compiler versions.

Improved Compilers
Improved Compilers refer to enhancements in compiler technology that address known issues.

Compiler Security Bugs
Compiler Security Bugs are vulnerabilities present in specific compiler versions that can lead to unexpected contract behavior.

Compiler Bugs And Overriding Requirements
Compiler Bugs and Overriding Requirements address how to handle compiler bugs and ensure compliance with security requirements.

Latest Compiler
The latest compiler version is recommended for use to mitigate risks associated with known vulnerabilities.

Solidity Compiler
Solidity Compiler is the specific compiler recommended to be updated to a recent version.

Solidity Release Notes
Solidity Release Notes document updates, bug fixes, and security improvements in Solidity compiler versions.

Etherscan Solidity Bug Info
Etherscan Solidity Bug Info provides information on known bugs in various Solidity compiler versions.

Version 0.4.13
Version 0.4.13 is an outdated version of the Solidity compiler that has known bugs and vulnerabilities.

Version 0.8.21
Version 0.8.21 is a more recent version of the Solidity compiler (as of August 2023), with numerous bug fixes compared to version 0.4.13.

OutdatedCompilerVersion Contract
The contract shows the pragma to use solidity version 0.4.13

M_required
M_required represents the number of confirmations required for an operation to be executed.<SEP>M_required is a public uint that represents the number of owners required to confirm an operation before it is executed.

M_numOwners
M_numOwners represents the number of owners in the multi-signature wallet contract.<SEP>M_numOwners is a public uint that represents the number of owners.

M_dailyLimit
M_dailyLimit represents the daily limit.<SEP>M_dailyLimit is a public uint representing the daily limit.

M_spentToday
M_spentToday is a public uint representing the amount spent today.

M_lastDay
M_lastDay represents last day<SEP>M_lastDay is a public uint representing the last day.

M_owners
M_owners is an array storing the addresses of the owners of the multi-signature wallet.<SEP>M_owners is a uint array of size 256 that stores the list of owners.

C_maxOwners
C_maxOwners represents the maximum number of owners allowed in the multi-signature wallet.<SEP>C_maxOwners is a constant uint equal to 250, representing the maximum number of owners.

M_ownerIndex
M_ownerIndex is a mapping that stores the index of each owner's address in the m_owners array.<SEP>M_ownerIndex is a mapping from uint to uint that serves as an index on the list of owners, allowing reverse lookup.

M_pending
M_pending is a mapping from bytes32 to PendingState, representing the ongoing operations.

M_pendingIndex
M_pendingIndex is an array of bytes32 representing the index of pending operations.

M_txs
M_txs is a mapping from bytes32 to Transaction, representing the pending transactions.

WalletLibrary
WalletLibrary is a Solidity contract implementing multi-signature wallet functionality.<SEP>The WalletLibrary has a constant address.<SEP>WalletLibrary is a contract with a constant address 0xcafecafecafecafecafecafecafecafecafecafe.

SimpleSuicide
SimpleSuicide is a contract with a function to selfdestruct.

SuicideMultiTxFeasible
SuicideMultiTxFeasible is a contract that can self-destruct if initialized is not zero.

SuicideMultiTxInfeasible
SuicideMultiTxInfeasible is a contract that can self-destruct if initialized is not equal to 2.

SELFDESTRUCT Instruction
The SELFDESTRUCT instruction allows a contract to destroy itself, potentially leading to vulnerabilities if not properly protected.

CWE-284
CWE-284 refers to Improper Access Control, a security weakness that can lead to unauthorized actions.

EthTrust Security Levels
EthTrust Security Levels are standards for Ethereum contract security.

Protect Self-destruction
Protect Self-destruction is an EthTrust Security Level requirement that focuses on securing the self-destruct functionality of a contract.

Enforce Least Privilege
Enforce Least Privilege is an EthTrust Security Level requirement that emphasizes limiting access rights to the minimum necessary.

Parity "I accidentally killed it" Bug
The Parity "I accidentally killed it" bug is a past incident where a vulnerability related to self-destruction resulted in significant consequences.

WalletLibrary.sol
WalletLibrary.sol is a sample Solidity smart contract that implements a multi-signature wallet.

Gav Wood
Gav Wood is listed as one of the authors of the Wallet contract.

WalletEvents
WalletEvents is a Solidity contract defining events related to wallet activities.

WalletAbi
WalletAbi is a Solidity contract defining the Application Binary Interface for wallet functionalities.

Multi-sig Scheme
A Multi-sig Scheme requires multiple parties to approve an action, increasing security.

Owner Index
Owner Index represents the index of an owner.<SEP>Owner Index refers to the index of an owner, used to track their confirmation status in transactions.

Daily Limit
Daily Limit is the maximum amount that can be spent within a single day.

Multiowned
Multiowned refers to a contract or system that requires multiple owners to authorize actions.

Today
Today refers to the current day.<SEP>Today represents the current day, used to track spending against the daily limit.

Msg.Data
Msg.data refers to the message data associated with a transaction, used for identifying function calls and parameters.

New Limit
New Limit is the updated daily limit that owners can set, subject to confirmation.

Spent Today
Spent Today is the amount that has already been spent within the current day.

M_NumOwners
M_NumOwners stores the number of owners.<SEP>M_numOwners refers to the number of owners in the multiowned contract.

InitWallet
InitWallet is the function to initialize the wallet with owners, required confirmations, and the daily limit.

Kill
Kill is a function that destroys the contract, sending all funds to a specified address.

Execute
Execute is the function to execute a transaction, either immediately or through the multisig process.

Block.Number
Block.number represents the current block number in the blockchain.

ConfirmationNeeded
ConfirmationNeeded is an event that is triggered when a transaction requires additional confirmations.

Create
Create is a function that creates a new contract with the given value and code.

InvalidJumpLabel
InvalidJumpLabel is a label to handle errors when contract creation fails.

Confirm
Confirm is a function that confirms a transaction based on its hash.

M_Txs
M_Txs is used to store transaction details.<SEP>M_txs refers to the map of pending transactions identified by their hash.

M_LastDay
M_LastDay stores the last day the spend limit was reset.<SEP>M_lastDay stores the index of the most recent day, used for comparing against the current day.

InitDaylimit
InitDaylimit is the function used to set the initial daily limit and the index of the present day.

ResetSpentToday
ResetSpentToday is the function used to reset the amount already spent today.

Only_Uninitialized
Only_uninitialized is a modifier that prevents the contract from being initialized if it is already initialized.

OnlyManyOwners
OnlyManyOwners is a modifier that restricts function access to a sufficient number of owners.

_To
_To refers to the recipient address of a transaction.

_Value
_Value is the amount of Ether being sent in a transaction.

_Data
_Data refers to the data payload being sent with a transaction.

Suicide
Suicide is a function that destroys the contract and sends its funds to a specified address.

SingleTransact
SingleTransact is an event that is triggered when a single transaction is executed.

MultiTransact
MultiTransact is an event that is triggered when a multi-signature transaction is executed.

Msg.Sender
Msg.Sender is the sender of the message.

Operation
Operation refers to a specific action or transaction that requires confirmation.

M_Pending
M_Pending stores information about pending operations.

M_PendingIndex
M_PendingIndex is an array that stores the indices of pending operations.

OwnerIndexBit
OwnerIndexBit represents a bit in a bitmap indicating whether an owner has confirmed an operation.

Confirmation
Confirmation is an event that occurs when a message sender confirms an operation.

M_Owners
M_Owners is an array storing the addresses of the owners.

M_OwnerIndex
M_OwnerIndex maps owner addresses to their respective indices in the m_owners array.

M_SpentToday
M_SpentToday tracks the amount spent today.

M_DailyLimit
M_DailyLimit is the daily spending limit.

M_Required
M_Required represents the number of confirmations needed for an operation.

_H
_H is a variable used as an index or identifier.

Data
Data refers to unspecified data being processed.

_operation
_operation refers to a specific action or transaction that requires confirmation from multiple owners.

OwnerIndex
OwnerIndex stores index of owner.

Pending
Pending stores pending operations.

_from
_from represents the current owner address to be changed.

_to
_to represents the new owner address to replace the current owner.

_owner
_owner represents address of owner.

_newRequired
_newRequired represents the new required number of confirmations.

_addr
_addr represents an address.

_limit
_limit represents a limit, possibly daily limit.

Address[] _owners
Address[] _owners is an array of addresses representing the owners to be initialized in the multi-signature wallet.

Uint _required
Uint _required is the minimum number of confirmations required for transactions.

Pending.OwnersDone
Owner Index is used to check the status of owner confirmations in pending transactions.

SetDailyLimit
Msg.data is used as input for SetDailyLimit, requiring owner confirmation via sha3.

ConfirmAndCheck
ConfirmAndCheck determines the Owner Index of the sender to verify their confirmation.

initMultiowned
initMultiowned initializes m_numOwners with the length of the _owners array plus one.

revoke
OwnerIndex is the index of the owner revoking the operation, used to ensure the revoker is an owner.

changeOwner
_from is the address of the owner being replaced in the multi-signature wallet.

addOwner
_owner is the address of the owner being added to the multi-signature wallet.

removeOwner
_owner is the address of the owner being removed from the multi-signature wallet.

changeRequirement
_newRequired is the new number of confirmations required for operations in the multi-signature wallet.

isOwner
_addr is checked to see if it matches owner

hasConfirmed
_operation to check confirmation on

initDaylimit
_limit is the limit for the day

Uninitialized Storage Pointer
An uninitialized local storage variable that can point to unexpected storage locations in a smart contract.

Cwe-824
Access of Uninitialized Pointer, a common weakness enumeration related to uninitialized variables.

Crypto Roulette
A solidity smart contract where a player can guess a number and win the contract's balance.

Game
A structure in the CryptoRoulette contract that stores the player's address and their guessed number.

Sigma Prime
An entity that published a blog post about uninitialized storage pointers in Solidity.

Thec00n
Thec00n is the author of the smart contract honeypots.
